behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM output quality and variability. A mocked or deterministic generator can pass while the production model still returns generic replies that miss thread-specific details.
behaviors.test_description: Run generation against a real LLM with a fixed incoming thread containing 3-4 concrete facts/questions. Assert the draft explicitly addresses at least two thread-specific points (semantic/keyword rubric) and fails if it is generic. Execute multiple runs to detect variability-related regressions.

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is typically wiring between draft parsing, KB lookup/retrieval, and contradiction detection logic. Pure unit mocks can miss retrieval/index/schema mismatches that cause missed flags.
behaviors.test_description: Use real local KB storage/index and real contradiction-check module. Provide a draft with known contradictory claims and KB entries that disprove them; assert contradictions are flagged with correct claim references. Avoid mocking retrieval or KB adapters.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: This is mainly a generation-quality behavior under missing context. Mocked generation will hide hallucination risk; only a real model run can expose whether disclaimer behavior is actually followed.
behaviors.test_description: Run with a real LLM and a thread requiring factual detail while retrieval returns no relevant KB entries. Assert the draft contains the configured uncertainty/disclaimer language and does not include unsupported concrete claims. Repeat across runs/prompts to catch stochastic fabrication.

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: Real failure usually occurs in cross-module state/event wiring between draft status, approval action, and send trigger. Unit tests of a single state function can miss unintended auto-send paths.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise workflow with real orchestration components and a test mail transport adapter. After draft creation and fact-check, assert no send call occurs before explicit approval event; then assert exactly one send occurs after approval.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly validates a multi-step agent pipeline including at least one real LLM-dependent step plus downstream checks and gating. Lower levels cannot reveal cross-step failures, state handoff issues, or agentic compounding errors.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end scenario on a real email thread fixture from the actual email source path, invoke real draft generation, run factual consistency check against KB, and verify approval gating UI/state before send eligibility. Assert artifacts and state transitions at each stage and final readiness only after approval.
